l1: Question 1 — When did the SAN connectivity issue first appear?
Question 2 — Is the problem affecting all LUNs or specific ones?
Question 3 — Are multiple servers experiencing the same issue?
Question 4 — Has zoning or masking been changed recently?
Question 5 — Is the issue intermittent or persistent?
Question 6 — Are applications reporting I/O errors or just disconnections?
l2: Advanced question 1 — Are there any fabric changes such as switch firmware upgrades?
Advanced question 2 — Is multipath I/O (MPIO) configured and functioning correctly?
Advanced question 3 — Are HBAs (Host Bus Adapters) showing errors or degraded status?
Advanced question 4 — Is the SAN part of a clustered environment?
Advanced question 5 — Are there performance drops before disconnections?
Advanced question 6 — Is the issue isolated to a single fabric path or multiple?

l1: Command or question 1 — Check Disk Management for missing or offline LUNs
Command or question 2 — Run PowerShell Get-Disk to confirm visibility
Command or question 3 — Verify MPIO configuration with Get-MSDSMGlobalDefaultLoadBalancePolicy
Command or question 4 — Review Event Viewer for storage-related warnings
Command or question 5 — Check HBA status in Device Manager
l2: Advanced command 1 — Run fcinfo.exe to list HBA ports and WWNs
Advanced command 2 — Use PowerShell Get-InitiatorPort to check FC initiator status
Advanced command 3 — Query SAN switch zoning configuration
Advanced command 4 — Run Get-MPIOPath to verify active paths
Advanced command 5 — Review SAN fabric logs for port errors or link flaps
Advanced command 6 — Check performance counters for disk latency and queue length

l1: Resolution step 1 — Restart the server and recheck LUN visibility
Resolution step 2 — Verify HBA drivers and firmware are up to date
Resolution step 3 — Ensure correct zoning and masking for the server WWN
Resolution step 4 — Re-scan disks in Disk Management
Resolution step 5 — Confirm MPIO is installed and configured properly
l2: Advanced fix 1 — Replace or reseat faulty HBA if persistent errors occur
Advanced fix 2 — Validate SAN switch zoning and reconfigure if incorrect
Advanced fix 3 — Run cluster validation tests if part of a failover cluster
Advanced fix 4 — Adjust load balancing policies for MPIO if uneven path usage
Advanced fix 5 — Escalate to SAN vendor if fabric instability or metadata corruption suspected
Advanced fix 6 — Perform firmware rollback if recent upgrade caused instability

l1: Export log — Save Event Viewer storage logs
Collect trace — Run Get-Disk and export results
Run command — Execute fcinfo.exe and save output
Step 4 — Capture screenshot of Disk Management showing LUNs
Step 5 — Collect OS build and patch level
l2: Advanced collection 1 — Export Get-InitiatorPort detailed report
Advanced collection 2 — Collect MPIO path configuration with Get-MPIOPath
Advanced collection 3 — Gather performance monitor counters for SAN traffic
Advanced collection 4 — Export SAN switch logs and zoning configuration
Advanced collection 5 — Collect firmware/driver versions for all HBAs
Advanced collection 6 — Gather cluster logs if part of a failover cluster

symptom_slug.title: SAN LUNs missing or connectivity unstable

steps.action: Run Get-Disk to confirm missing LUNs

steps.expect: WWN not zoned or path errors detected

steps.action: Update HBA drivers and reconfigure zoning
Run Get-MPIOPath and monitor Event Viewer
steps.expect: Healthy multipath configuration with no new errors
